steps.title: Set up the weighted means
steps.content: Each mean is found by multiplying each value by its frequency, adding those products, and dividing by the total number of values. Both data sets have $2+4+5+7+9=27$ values, so compare the weighted sums before dividing by $27$.

steps.title: Compute data set A by hand
Compute data set B by hand
steps.content: The weighted sum for data set B is $$30(9)+34(7)+38(5)+42(4)+46(2)=270+238+190+168+92=958.$$ Since data set B also has $27$ values, $$\mu_B=\frac{958}{27}\approx35.48.$$

steps.title: Compare the means and select the correct choice
steps.content: Both data sets have the same number of values, so comparing means is the same as comparing their weighted sums. Since $1{,}094>958$, data set A has the greater mean. That matches choice A.
